response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack and web engineer with a strong track record at top-tier companies, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The most critical disqualifier is the absence of native-level Japanese proficiency, which is a non-negotiable listed requirement. Beyond that, his expertise is concentrated in TypeScript, Node.js, and web-oriented stacks with no demonstrated experience in embedded networking, security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), or Linux kernel development. His C experience is limited to early-career IDE tooling work rather than sustained systems or firmware development.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, and some embedded contexts, demonstrating strong general engineering fundamentals
Early-career C and C++ exposure at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and a Linux-based wave field synthesis thesis project show some low-level foundations
Strong background in developer tooling, CI/CD, and software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development) that would transfer to any engineering environment
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — English, Romanian, and basic German only, making this a hard disqualification for the role
Zero demonstrable experience with the core security technologies required: MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, device attack/tamper detection, or encryption at the firmware level
No evidence of Linux kernel investigation or source-code modification, embedded networking, L2/L3 switching, or chip-vendor SDK work — the entire technical domain of the role is absent

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security protocol experience
Extensive software engineering tenure and systems exposure
